goal: Land `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/chunker.rs` with a `Chunker` type that emits AST-aware code chunks (master plan Â§12.2 line 1339: one chunk per function/method/class, never splits mid-function, 512-token cap). Closes feature P1-W2-F03 and keeps the `ucil-treesitter` crate frozen at `parser.rs` + `symbols.rs` + `tag_cache.rs` + `chunker.rs` per the master-plan directory layout (Â§14 line 1648).
plan_summary: Add a new sibling module `chunker.rs` to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/` alongside the frozen `parser.rs` (WO-0005), `symbols.rs` (WO-0017), and `tag_cache.rs` (WO-0018). The module exposes: (1) a `ChunkError` enum via `thiserror` â€” `#[non_exhaustive]` with variants for line-range / UTF-8 boundary issues (intentionally minimal â€” tree-sitter already produced a valid tree); (2) a `Chunk` struct matching the master-plan Â§12.2 code_chunks_schema fields (`id`, `file_path`, `language`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `content`, `symbol_name`, `symbol_kind`, `token_count`) â€” derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize`; (3) a stateless `Chunker` struct with `Chunker::chunk(&self, tree, source, file_path, language) -> Result<Vec<Chunk>, ChunkError>`. Implementation: for extractable languages (Rust, Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, Go â€” the 5 covered by `SymbolExtractor`), call `SymbolExtractor::extract` to get per-symbol line ranges, then for each `Function / Method / Class / Struct / Enum / Trait / Interface` symbol emit one chunk (classes AND their nested methods both emit â€” overlap is intentional per master-plan 'complete function/method/class'). For fallback languages (Java, C, C++, Ruby, Bash, JSON) walk the parse tree's root child nodes via `tree_sitter::TreeCursor` and emit one chunk per top-level AST node (symbol_name + symbol_kind left `None`). Oversized chunks (token_count > 512) collapse to a signature-only chunk: `signature` (from `ExtractedSymbol.signature` when present, else first non-blank source line) + first paragraph of `doc_comment` (if any, split at the first blank-line within the doc text). Token count uses a byte-based estimate `max(1, ceil(content.len() / 4))` â€” OpenAI's standard English-language heuristic; acceptable here because the `tiktoken`-grade tokenizer is scoped to ucil-embeddings in Phase 3 (master-plan Â§14 line 1657). `id` is formatted `{file_path_display}:{start_line}:{end_line}` per Â§12.2. Module-root flat tests per DEC-0005 â€” NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `chunker.rs` â€” so the frozen nextest selector `chunker::` resolves every test as `ucil_treesitter::chunker::<test_name>`. No new workspace deps â€” `SymbolExtractor`, `Language`, and `tree_sitter::Tree` are all already on the crate's dep graph from WO-0005 / WO-0017. The `serde` dev-dep-free path for the `Language` field re-uses the existing `language_serde` module inside `symbols.rs` via a sibling `#[serde(with = ...)]` declaration that imports it (`use crate::symbols::language_serde_for_chunker;` â€” see scope_in; if cross-module Serde sharing is non-obvious, executor may duplicate the 40-line `language_serde` mod inside `chunker.rs` rather than touch `symbols.rs` â€” frozen).
scope_in: crates/ucil-treesitter/src/chunker.rs (new module, ~500-650 lines incl. 12 tests + rustdoc)

* Module-level rustdoc citing master-plan Â§12.2 line 1339 (AST-aware chunking spec), Â§18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1734 (ucil-treesitter scope), Â§14 line 1648 (directory layout), and Â§12.2 code_chunks_schema for the Chunk struct field mapping
* `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` IF pedantic flags `Chunker` / `ChunkError` inside the `chunker` module (same precedent as parser.rs / symbols.rs / tag_cache.rs)

* `ChunkError` enum via `thiserror`, `#[non_exhaustive]` â€” minimum variants: `InvalidLineRange { start: u32, end: u32 }` (when extracted symbol's end_line < start_line) and `Utf8Boundary(std::str::Utf8Error)` (if a byte-range slice lands mid-codepoint) â€” use `#[from]` on the Utf8Error variant; `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]`

* `pub struct Chunk` with fields (exact order): `pub id: String`, `pub file_path: PathBuf`, `pub language: Language`, `pub start_line: u32`, `pub end_line: u32`, `pub content: String`, `pub symbol_name: Option<String>`, `pub symbol_kind: Option<SymbolKind>`, `pub token_count: u32` â€” derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize`; the `language` field reuses a Serde adapter (see plan_summary â€” either import `crate::symbols::language_serde` if visible, or duplicate the 40-line module; do NOT make `symbols.rs` grow a `pub` export because symbols.rs is frozen by WO-0017)
* `pub const MAX_TOKENS: u32 = 512` â€” master plan Â§12.4 line 2030 `chunk_max_tokens = 512`
* `const BYTES_PER_TOKEN: usize = 4` â€” token-count heuristic constant, private to the module
* `pub struct Chunker` â€” unit struct, stateless, `#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, Copy)]`
* `Chunker::new() -> Self` â€” `const fn`, `#[must_use]`

* `Chunker::chunk(&self, tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, source: &str, file_path: &Path, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<Chunk>, ChunkError>` â€” main entry; `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.treesitter.chunk", level = "debug", skip(self, tree, source))]` per master-plan Â§15.2 span-naming convention

* Private helper `chunk_from_symbol(sym: &ExtractedSymbol, source: &str, file_path: &Path, language: Language) -> Result<Chunk, ChunkError>` â€” builds one `Chunk` from an `ExtractedSymbol`: slices `source` by line range (inclusive on both ends), counts tokens, applies the oversize-signature-only fallback if `token_count > MAX_TOKENS`

* Private helper `chunk_from_ast_node(node: &tree_sitter::Node<'_>, source: &str, file_path: &Path, language: Language) -> Result<Chunk, ChunkError>` â€” builds one `Chunk` from a top-level AST node (used by the fallback-language branch); `symbol_name` and `symbol_kind` are `None`

* Private helper `slice_lines(source: &str, start_line: u32, end_line: u32) -> Result<String, ChunkError>` â€” slices `source` to the inclusive 1-based line range, returning the sub-string with trailing newline stripped; errors on `end_line < start_line`
* Private helper `count_tokens(content: &str) -> u32` â€” returns `max(1, ceil(content.len() / BYTES_PER_TOKEN))` cast to `u32`

* Private helper `signature_only_chunk_content(sym: &ExtractedSymbol, source: &str) -> String` â€” returns `sym.signature.clone().unwrap_or_else(first_non_blank_line)` + (`\n\n` + `first_doc_paragraph(sym.doc_comment)` when doc_comment is `Some`)

* Private helper `first_doc_paragraph(doc: &str) -> &str` â€” returns everything up to the first blank-line (regex-free: scan for `

` or `
\s*
`)

* Private helper `walk_top_level_chunk_worthy_nodes<'tree>(tree: &'tree tree_sitter::Tree) -> Vec<tree_sitter::Node<'tree>>` â€” returns the direct children of the root node; filters out trivial nodes (e.g. `comment`, `whitespace`, kind ending in `_list`) only if they match a well-defined skip-list; otherwise includes everything

* Private helper `make_chunk_id(file_path: &Path, start_line: u32, end_line: u32) -> String` â€” returns `format!("{}:{}:{}", file_path.display(), start_line, end_line)` matching master-plan Â§12.2 line 1325
crates/ucil-treesitter/src/lib.rs â€” add `pub mod chunker;` + `pub use chunker::{Chunk, ChunkError, Chunker};` alongside the existing parser/symbols/tag_cache re-exports
Module-root tests in `chunker.rs` (flat â€” NOT under `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005). Required tests (exact names, prefix-match the frozen selector `chunker::`):

* `chunker_emits_chunk_per_rust_function` â€” parses a Rust source with 3 top-level fns; asserts 3 chunks returned, each with `symbol_kind == Some(SymbolKind::Function)` and correct `symbol_name` and non-empty `content`

* `chunker_emits_chunk_per_python_class_and_method` â€” parses a Python source with 1 class containing 2 methods; asserts â‰¥ 3 chunks (the class + its 2 methods) â€” class chunk kind Class, method chunks kind Method, method chunks nested inside class chunk's line range
* `chunker_emits_chunk_per_typescript_class_and_interface` â€” parses a TypeScript source with 1 class and 1 interface; asserts one chunk per and correct SymbolKind tagging (Class, Interface)
* `chunker_emits_chunk_per_go_func_and_type` â€” parses a Go source with 1 func + 1 struct; asserts 2 chunks tagged Function and Struct

* `chunker_oversized_function_becomes_signature_only_chunk` â€” synthesises a Rust fn with a body > 2048 bytes (~ >512 tokens); asserts the emitted chunk's `token_count` is â‰¤ `MAX_TOKENS`, its `content` starts with the function signature line (e.g. `fn huge(`), and it does NOT contain the 2048-byte body filler

* `chunker_oversized_function_with_doc_comment_keeps_first_paragraph` â€” synthesises a Rust fn with a multi-paragraph doc comment AND an oversize body; asserts the signature-only chunk's `content` contains the first doc paragraph (separated by blank line from the signature) but NOT the second paragraph

* `chunker_id_format_matches_file_and_line_range` â€” for any emitted chunk, asserts `chunk.id == format!("{}:{}:{}", chunk.file_path.display(), chunk.start_line, chunk.end_line)` â€” property test across â‰¥ 3 symbols
* `chunker_language_field_populated_correctly` â€” for each of Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go: asserts every chunk's `language` equals the input `Language`

* `chunker_symbol_name_and_kind_none_for_fallback_language_top_level` â€” parses a Java source with 1 class; asserts at least one chunk is emitted and every chunk has `symbol_name == None` AND `symbol_kind == None` (fallback-language branch)
* `chunker_empty_source_returns_empty_vec` â€” passes `""` as source; asserts `chunk(...)? == vec![]`
* `chunker_token_count_matches_byte_estimate` â€” builds a chunk from a known-length source; asserts `token_count == max(1, ceil(content.len() / 4))`

* `chunker_chunks_never_split_mid_function` â€” parses a Rust source with 1 long fn; asserts the chunk's content begins at `fn <name>` and ends at the matching closing brace (brace-balance check on the slice) â€” no interior cut

* `chunker_line_ranges_are_well_formed` â€” across every emitted chunk: `start_line >= 1`, `end_line >= start_line`, `end_line <= total_lines(source)` â€” property test over a multi-symbol Rust source

Rustdoc on every public item: `Chunker`, `Chunker::new`, `Chunker::chunk`, `Chunk`, `ChunkError`, `MAX_TOKENS` â€” module-level rustdoc cites master-plan Â§12.2 line 1339, Â§18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1734, Â§14 line 1648 (directory layout), and documents the byte-based token-count heuristic + signature-only oversize fallback.
scope_out: Adding a real tokenizer (tiktoken, HuggingFace BPE, SentencePiece) â€” the byte-based heuristic is intentional; `ucil-embeddings`'s Phase 3 chunker (master-plan Â§14 line 1657) owns the real tokenizer
Sliding-window chunking of oversize functions â€” master-plan Â§12.2 line 1339 explicitly prescribes `signature + first-paragraph doc comment` for oversize; do NOT add sliding-window splitting
Cross-file chunking (whole-repo scan) â€” this WO's `chunk()` operates per-file; the daemon's file-watcher orchestrator is the whole-repo loop and lives in P1-W4-F04
Persistence of chunks to LanceDB / SQLite â€” phase 2 territory (master-plan Â§13 Week 6)
Embedding generation â€” phase 3 territory (master-plan Â§18 Phase 3 Week 10-11)
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` â€” frozen by WO-0005
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs` â€” frozen by WO-0017 (executor may `use` symbols.rs items but not edit)
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/tag_cache.rs` â€” frozen by WO-0018
Any modification to `crates/ucil-core/**` â€” ChunkError stays local; no bridge to a central error type
Adding a `ucil-daemon` dependency edge to `ucil-treesitter` â€” phase-log invariant 5 forbids it
Adding new workspace deps â€” the existing tree-sitter, serde, thiserror, tracing, streaming-iterator deps already cover every need
Integration with the hot-staging / file-watcher path â€” wiring is a later WO

Any new ADR â€” master-plan Â§12.2 line 1339 is unambiguous, the `Chunker` owns AST-aware chunking, and the 512-token cap is named in Â§12.4 line 2030. Raise a proposed ADR ONLY if a real ambiguity or build failure surfaces
acceptance: New `chunker` module exists at `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/chunker.rs` and is declared + re-exported from `lib.rs`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter chunker::` matches â‰¥ 10 tests, all passing (the P1-W2-F03 acceptance selector)
`cargo clippy -p ucil-treesitter --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery, inherits from lib.rs)
`cargo doc -p ucil-treesitter --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: stashing `chunker.rs` causes the selector `chunker::` to return 0 matching tests (or cargo build fails); popping restores â€” `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W2-F03`
No mocks of `tree_sitter::Tree`, `Parser`, or `SymbolExtractor` â€” tests build real trees via `Parser::new().parse(...)` and then call `Chunker::chunk(&tree, ...)`
`parser.rs` byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` against `origin/main` shows 0 lines on that path)
`symbols.rs` byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` against `origin/main` shows 0 lines on that path)
`tag_cache.rs` byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` against `origin/main` shows 0 lines on that path)
Module-root tests are flat â€” no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `chunker.rs` â€” per DEC-0005
No new `[workspace.dependencies]` entries in the root `Cargo.toml`
Token-count cap holds: every emitted chunk has `token_count <= 512`
id format matches schema: every emitted chunk's `id == format!("{}:{}:{}", file_path.display(), start_line, end_line)`
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter 'chunker::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 10)}'

master-plan:Â§12.4 line 2030 (chunk_max_tokens = 512 â€” authoritative token cap)
master-plan:Â§14 line 1648 (ucil-treesitter directory layout: lib.rs, parser.rs, symbols.rs, chunker.rs â€” and Â§14 line 1657 for the sibling ucil-embeddings chunker scoped to Phase 3)
master-plan:Â§18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1734 ('Implement ucil-treesitter: multi-language parser, symbol extraction, AST-aware chunking')
master-plan:Â§15.2 Tracing span naming convention (ucil.<layer>.<op>) â€” span name ucil.treesitter.chunk
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (flat module-root tests for selector robustness â€” critical here)
work-order:WO-0017-treesitter-symbol-extraction (predecessor â€” defines ExtractedSymbol, SymbolKind, SymbolExtractor; frozen; Chunker depends on it)
work-order:WO-0018-treesitter-tag-cache (sibling â€” establishes the flat-module-root / rustdoc / pedantic-clippy / span-naming pattern this WO mirrors)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; no unsafe without ADR; one crate per Cargo.toml; edition 2021)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant 5: ucil-treesitter depends only on ucil-core + tree-sitter crates; Â§Features in scope lists P1-W2-F03 'AST-aware chunker (â‰¤512 token chunks at function boundaries)')
lessons_applied: Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 â€” the frozen selector `chunker::` is a path prefix; placing tests flat mirrors `tag_cache.rs` (WO-0018) and `symbols.rs` (WO-0017), avoiding the WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch failure mode
No mocks of tree-sitter types (phase-log invariant 1) â€” tests build real trees via `Parser::new().parse(...)` and call `Chunker::chunk(&tree, ...)`; no `mockall`, no stub `Tree` shim
Do NOT modify `parser.rs` / `symbols.rs` / `tag_cache.rs` â€” frozen by prior WOs; `git diff == 0` acceptance checks enforce byte-for-byte preservation
Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/**` â€” chunker's error is local; no bridge to a central error type
Do NOT add a `ucil-daemon` dependency edge â€” phase-log invariant 5; `! grep -q 'ucil-daemon' Cargo.toml` acceptance check enforces

Language Serde adapter: if cross-module reuse from `symbols::language_serde` (currently `mod language_serde { ... }` private to `symbols.rs`) is tricky without editing symbols.rs, DUPLICATE the 40-line adapter inside `chunker.rs` rather than promote the existing one â€” symbols.rs is frozen
Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W2-F03`, `Work-order: WO-0019` trailers on every commit; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push

If the module file exceeds ~400 lines (likely â€” 12 tests + rustdoc + per-language helpers), the DEC-0001 / DEC-0005 module-introduction-commit precedent permits coherent commits up to ~550 lines when splitting would produce dead-code intermediate states

Session-abandonment prevention (cf. RCA for WO-0012): before ending the session, verify that (a) at least one `.rs` diff against `origin/main` exists on the feature branch, (b) every commit has been pushed, AND (c) `0019-ready-for-review.md` has been written â€” if any is missing, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the blocker

Mutation-oracle survival (cf. WO-0017 / WO-0018 pattern): if `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W2-F03` fails because stashing `chunker.rs` leaves behind tests that still pass (e.g. integration tests in a separate file), add a compile-time safeguard in `chunker.rs` itself so that stashing the body necessarily breaks the selector
Oversize fallback is master-plan-prescribed: `signature + first-paragraph doc comment` ONLY. Do NOT invent sliding-window splitting â€” raise an ADR if a business case for it emerges

Token heuristic is byte-based (`max(1, ceil(len / 4))`) â€” NOT a real tokenizer. The real tokenizer lives in `ucil-embeddings::chunker` (Phase 3). Do NOT pull in `tiktoken` / `tokenizers` / `sentencepiece` here
